description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the Docker image $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/qemu/$IMAGE:$QEMU_CI_CONTAINER_TAG for all jobs and defines a single build stage. Add a cross_accel_build_job in the build stage that runs a before_script sourcing scripts/ci/gitlab-ci-section, sets JOBS=$(expr $(nproc) + 1), caches the ccache/ directory, exports CCACHE_BASEDIR, CCACHE_DIR, CCACHE_MAXSIZE, updates PATH, creates a build directory, runs ../configure --enable-werror --disable-docs $QEMU_CONFIGURE_OPTS --disable-tools --enable-${ACCEL:-kvm} $EXTRA_CONFIGURE_OPTS, then make -j"$JOBS" all check-build, and if $MAKE_CHECK_ARGS is set runs $MAKE -j"$JOBS" $MAKE_CHECK_ARGS; add a cross_system_build_job with the same initial steps, additionally cats /packages.txt, zeroes ccache stats, configures QEMU with system targets and a long exclude list, builds and tests as above, then if an installer is produced runs make installer, renames the .exe with the git version, and finally shows ccache stats, caching ccache, with an 80m timeout; add a cross_user_build_job that configures QEMU for user‑mode only (disables system, excludes user targets), builds and tests similarly, caches ccache, and has a 60m timeout. Attach the .cross_test_artifacts definition to the build jobs so that build/meson-logs/testlog.txt and build/meson-logs/testlog.junit.xml are uploaded as artifacts that expire in 7 days and are always retained.
